violations.text: The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Staff restocked paper towels during the time of this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

Container was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Staff removed container from sink at time of inspection. Proper hand sink usages reviewed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. Staff relocated can opener to 3-bay sink to be washed, rinsed and sanitized. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

Several foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

Working containers of (various dry food ingredients) are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026
Floor located in the walk-in cooler was not in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026
The condensate from the (walk-in cooler) is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

The floor behind door in dry storage area and underneath mop sink has an accumulation of debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026
Mop observed not properly air drying. Mops must be allowed to air dry after use. Staff place mop to air dry. Proper mop air drying reviewed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026
An allergen awareness statement was not available for customer viewing at the point of sale/menu board. An allergen awareness statement is to be provided. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025

Several Food items in the Large Prep Unit were observed at 42 to 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Food items observed at 44 degrees Fahrenheit or below were moved to colder refrigeration, and food items observed at 45 degrees Fahrenheit and higher were voluntarily disposed of by the PIC at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025

The back door to the outside, located in the back-room area, is not tight-fitting (gap at the base). Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025

At the time of inspection, the large prep unit was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 38 degrees Fahrenheit, but food items, in cooler since previous day, were all observed to be at 42 to 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Unit is to be adjusted and monitored for proper cold holding temperatures prior to use for storage of time/temperature control for safety foods. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025

The following areas of the facility were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue, dust, and debris... - The ceiling air vents in the front and back areas - The floor under/behind the fryolators The physical facilities shall be cleaned and maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025

The following areas of the facility were observed in need of repair... - The walk-in cooler floor was observed to be lifting and in need of repair - The ceiling over the walk-in cooler was observed to be missing tiles and need replacing The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the kitchen area. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025
